reviewNotes: Prefer fewer stronger authoring targets: merge broad or thin concepts into existing backup, patch management, partition/formatting, UEFI, and installation planning concepts.
Windows 11 requirements and lifecycle policy require vendor verification before final authoring.
Several tool objects are acceptable as brief Knowledge Objects if the platform supports command/tool entries.

conceptDecisions.title: Operating System Upgrade
conceptDecisions.reason: The manifest treats general upgrade as a broad lead-in to in-place upgrade. Preserve the comparison and preservation guidance, but avoid a separate duplicate Knowledge Object.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: In-Place Upgrade
conceptDecisions.reason: Strongly supported, core lesson concept that contrasts directly with clean install and belongs in OS maintenance planning.
mustCover: Preserves applications, documents, settings, user accounts, and existing OS context.
Usually launched from inside the existing operating system.
Best when data, applications, or specialized configurations should remain in place.
Still requires backup and compatibility planning.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Clean Install
conceptDecisions.reason: Strongly supported, core lesson concept with clear consequences, procedure distinction, and backup/partition risk.
mustCover: Removes existing OS files, applications, user data, and settings.
Usually starts by booting from installation media.
Requires backup and drive/partition review before proceeding.
Use when a fresh system state is desired.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Backup Before OS Installation
conceptDecisions.reason: This is a contextual use case of backup before destructive OS work, not a separate standalone concept.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Partitioning and Formatting During Installation
conceptDecisions.reason: The installation-specific material should enrich the existing partitioning/formatting concept rather than create a duplicate.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: OS Hardware Compatibility Check
conceptDecisions.reason: Clear pre-upgrade planning concept with strong source support and broad relevance across OS installation and upgrade decisions.
mustCover: Check memory, drive space, processor support, and feature support.
Use vendor documentation and compatibility tools.
Run checks before upgrade or installation.
Incompatibility may require upgrades or replacement.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

notes: Reject or revise any relationship whose direction implies the upgrade procedure is a prerequisite for the compatibility check; the check should inform the upgrade decision.

conceptDecisions.title: Windows PC Health Check
conceptDecisions.reason: Specific Windows readiness tool is supported by the manifest and useful as a linked tool object, but should remain brief.
mustCover: PC Health Check checks Windows 11 readiness.
It reports which requirements pass or fail.
It can identify missing or disabled Secure Boot or TPM 2.0.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Application and Driver Compatibility
conceptDecisions.reason: Worth authoring because incompatible apps and drivers are a practical upgrade risk; enrich with realistic troubleshooting context.
mustCover: Applications and device drivers may not support the newer OS.
Check vendor/developer documentation before upgrading.
Compatibility problems can block or complicate upgrades.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Windows Product Lifecycle
conceptDecisions.reason: Lifecycle calendars and support retirement are central to upgrade planning; current dates must be verified during authoring.
mustCover: Lifecycle calendars show support and retirement dates.
Lifecycle status affects upgrade planning.
Quality and feature updates are tied to lifecycle support.
Verify current Microsoft lifecycle details before publishing.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Quality Updates
conceptDecisions.reason: Quality updates are only lightly covered and fit best under broader patch/update management unless the curriculum later models Windows update types separately.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Feature Updates
conceptDecisions.reason: Feature updates are mentioned as an update type and should enrich patch/update management rather than become a standalone object here.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Windows 11 System Requirements
conceptDecisions.reason: High-value Windows readiness topic, but exact requirements are vendor-maintained and should be verified against current Microsoft documentation before Knowledge Authoring.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Trusted Platform Module 2.0
conceptDecisions.reason: Strongly supported security/hardware concept with direct Windows 11, BitLocker, and Windows Hello relationships.
mustCover: TPM is hardware-backed cryptographic capability.
TPM 2.0 or later is required for Windows 11 in this lesson context.
TPM can support BitLocker and Windows Hello.
TPM may need to be supported and enabled.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: TPM Management Console Snap-in
conceptDecisions.reason: Useful Windows command/tool object for checking TPM details; should link to TPM without duplicating the full TPM explanation.
mustCover: tpm.msc opens TPM management details.
It is an MMC snap-in.
Use it to verify TPM status/version/readiness.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: UEFI Firmware
conceptDecisions.reason: UEFI should already be represented from firmware lessons; this source should enrich it with Windows 11 and Secure Boot context.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Secure Boot
conceptDecisions.reason: Strongly supported security control and Windows 11 readiness requirement; check for an existing Secure Boot object before final authoring.
mustCover: Secure Boot is required for Windows 11 in this lesson context.
Secure Boot is tied to UEFI firmware.
Status can be checked in System Information under System Summary.
Enabled/on is the desired readiness state.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Windows System Information Utility
conceptDecisions.reason: A useful Windows tool object for checking Secure Boot State and system summary details.
mustCover: System Information can inspect System Summary details.
Secure Boot State is checked there.
Secure Boot should be on for Windows 11 readiness.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: Installation Planning Questions
conceptDecisions.reason: Good checklist-style procedure concept that consolidates target drive, partition plan, license keys, backup, and compatibility checks.
mustCover: Confirm target drive.
Plan partition configuration.
Have license keys available.
Check data, partitions, backups, requirements, apps, and drivers before proceeding.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

conceptDecisions.title: User Preferences Preservation
conceptDecisions.reason: Too thin for standalone authoring; preserve as a checklist note under installation planning and backup scope.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement is appropriate for the reviewed concept and should be preserved unless the target concept already exists in another canonical module.

enrichmentQueue.title: Windows 11 System Requirements
neededEvidenceOrContext: Current PC Health Check behavior or Microsoft guidance.
Any edition, version, or supported processor nuance relevant to A+ authoring.
enrichmentQueue.reason: The concept is important, but exact requirement details are vendor-maintained and should not be authored from the transcript alone.

gapReview.reason: The lesson depends on installation media without teaching it.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Link to an existing installation media concept/procedure or create one if absent.

gapReview.reason: Backup is emphasized but scope and verification are not explained.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Enrich backup and installation planning with backup scope, verification, user profile data, and preference capture.

gapReview.reason: Exact Windows 11 requirements must be verified against Microsoft before authoring.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Send Windows 11 requirements to enrichment before Knowledge Authoring.

gapReview.reason: TPM purpose is only briefly introduced.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Author TPM at normal depth and link to BitLocker and Windows Hello without expanding those features here.

gapReview.reason: Lifecycle timing and support status change over time.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Verify Microsoft lifecycle terminology and dates during authoring; avoid hardcoding transcript-only support windows.

globalReviewNotes: Do not recreate Transcript Intelligence during review.
Do not over-author a complete Windows installation walkthrough from this lesson alone.
Normalize the source phrase 'UEFI BIOS' carefully as UEFI firmware in the canonical knowledge base.
Preserve upgrade-versus-clean-install decision-making as the main lesson outcome.
